user.title: AWS Bedrock Configuration
user.description: Configure your AWS Bedrock connection. You need AWS credentials with Bedrock access.

model.title: Select Bedrock Model
model.description: Pick a Claude inference profile available in your AWS account and region. The list is fetched live via bedrock:ListInferenceProfiles; a built-in list is used as a fallback.

error.invalid_credentials: Invalid AWS credentials. Please check your access key and secret key.
error.access_denied: Access denied. Please ensure your IAM user has bedrock:InvokeModel permissions.
error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to AWS Bedrock. Check your region and credentials.

data.prompt: System prompt template. Edit the text freely; two placeholders are substituted automatically: <current_date> inserts the current date and time, <devices> inserts the list of exposed entities with their state. Leaving them in is recommended; remove them to opt out of that context.
data.auto_attach_cameras: Attach snapshots from exposed cameras to every conversation turn (requires a vision-capable model; adds ~1.5K input tokens per image per turn)
data.expose_areas_only: Restrict the device list in the system prompt to entities in these areas. Empty = every exposed entity (default). Massive token savings on large homes.
data.device_prompt_mode: How the device list is formatted. 'full' = current format with state + attributes. 'compact' = name/entity/area/state, no attributes. 'names_only' = just name/entity/area (biggest token savings; the model won't know current states).
data.max_prompt_tokens: Soft cap on the rendered device list size in tokens (rough heuristic). 0 = no cap.
data.llm_hass_api: Home Assistant LLM API for device control
